bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0), and the opponent's starting offer yields minimal utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and protect my high-value issues, I will counter with my optimal offer. There is no need to make concessions this early in the process.

To reciprocate, show flexibility, and explore their preferences, I will concede one step on my least important issue (issueD), changing it from valueA to valueB, while holding firm on my most highly valued issues.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.04). The opponent has made a small concession on issueA but is repeating their previous offer overall, maintaining values that yield very low utility for me on all other issues. To prompt further movement while protecting my highest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB), I will maintain my positions there and make a slight concession on issueC, reflecting a gradual, utility-maximizing approach.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06), and the opponent has been repeatedly offering the same near-minimal utility bundle. To encourage flexibility without sacrificing much value, I will continue to make small, steady concessions on my lower-priority issues (Issue C and Issue D), while firmly holding the line on my top-priority issues (Issue A and Issue B). This maintains my overall utility comfortably above 0.93.

The current time is very early (t=0.08) in the negotiation. The opponent is using a tough strategy and has literally reverted to their worst possible offer of all 'valueF's. I am responding by conceding slowly and primarily on my lowest importance issues (issueC and issueD), keeping my most important issues (issueA and issueB) at their maximum values to maintain a high utility bid.

The current time is early in the negotiation (t=0.10) and the opponent is holding very firm with their offers, providing only my least desired values. To keep the process moving, I will concede slightly on issues C and D where my importance weights are lowest (0.2 and 0.1). I will maintain my highest preferred values for issues A and B, which hold the bulk of my utility.

The opponent is holding very firm and maintaining extremely low utility for me across all issues. Given it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), I will maintain a strong position on my highest weighted issues (issueA and issueB) while making minor, measured concessions on my less important issues (issueC and issueD) to demonstrate a willingness to compromise without giving up too much value prematurely. I am targeting a utility of roughly 0.89.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.14). Given their aggressive strategy and the ample time remaining, I will maintain my positions on my most heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB), while continuing to make slow, incremental concessions on my least important issue (issueD) to demonstrate a willingness to negotiate.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.16), but the opponent is extremely rigid, repeating variations of their absolute best value while offering me minimal utility. Since my previous concessions on my low-weight issues (C and D) haven't prompted a shift, I will make a cautious concession on issueB (my second-highest priority) to signal a willingness to compromise and see if they reciprocate, while maintaining an overall high utility target of 0.840 for myself.

The negotiation is still in the early phase (t=0.18), but the opponent is anchoring extremely hard, alternating between the lowest and second-lowest utility values for my most important issue (issueA) and offering only the lowest utility value for all others. I will continue to make small, careful concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueC and issueD) while holding firm on my high-priority issues (issueA and issueB) to encourage the opponent to make meaningful movement.

The opponent is remaining rigid, insisting largely on valueF for all issues. To encourage movement while protecting my most critical interests, I will concede entirely on my least important issue (issueD) by offering their preferred value, valueF. In exchange, I hold firm on issueA and issueB, preserving a utility above 0.80 for myself since we are still early in the negotiation.

I noticed the opponent is holding very firm to their initial positions, mostly demanding valueE or valueF for all issues. Because `time_fraction` is only 0.22, I will make a gradual concession on my less important issues (Issue C) and keep offering the opponent's preferred value for my least important issue (Issue D) while holding strong on issues that are highly valuable to me (A and B).
